raw_consumer_statement: I noticed the amount due on my statement was higher than expected and seemed too high. I audited the charges on the statement ending XX/XX/2024. The balance due was {$11000.00} higher than the actual charges verified in my audit. I called Amex and verified all the charges with the Amex agent. I asked her to explain the discrepancy between actual charges and the amount due on the statement. She gave a verbal response regarding policies, procedures, and credits. I did not understand. I invited her to go the the math, line by line to explain the amount due in dollars, not words. She refused to do it and cut me off in the middle of my verbal requests for information and attempts to explain my position. I asked for a supervisor and a new agent was passed to me. We went had the same conversation, including my invitation to do the math line by line. She refused to go through the math and cut me off while I was speaking. I don't recall ever completing a full statement. I asked for her supervisor and was passed to t a third agent. This agent cut me off before I completed a single statement and refused to go through the math, line by line, with me to explain the amount due. All three agents were disrespectful. After continuing to ask for the third agent to respect me by letting me complete a verbal statement and asking her why she continued to disrespect me, she hung up on me. Supposedly, all of this is recorded and on record. I am willing to pay all money I owe to Amex if they can explain it to me in dollars with math, not words. Until I understand why I owe what I owe, I refuse to pay my current billing statement. I have performed my due diligence in trying to resolve this directly with Amex with a recorded conversation that lasted over two hours with three different agents. I am requesting that CFPB work with Amex to explain how Amex determined how much I owe and explain the discrepancies with math, numbers, and dollars.
